q:
listen
 'What did he say?' 'I don't know. I ___.'
a: I wasn't listening.

q:
sit
 We ___ at the back of the theatre. We couldn't hear very well.
a: We were sitting at the back of the theatre.

q:
work
 This time last year Steve ___ on a farm.
a: Steve was working on a farm.

q:
look
 They didn't see me. They ___ in my direction.
a: They weren't looking in my direction.

q:
snow
 The weather was bad. It was very cold and it ___.
a: It was snowing.

q:
go
 I saw you in your car. Where ___?
a: Where were you going?

q:
look
 I saw Kate a few minutes ago. She ___ for you.
a: She was looking for you.

q:
wait
 When I got to the cafe, my friends ___ for me.
a: My friends were waiting for me.

q:
watch
 We fell asleep while we ___ a film.
a: We were watching a film.

q:
live
 Amy learnt Italian when she ___ in Rome.
a: Amy learnt Italian when she was living in Rome.

q:
feel
 Tom didn't come out with us because he ___ well.
a: Tom didn't come out with us because he wasn't feeling well.

q:
drive
 The car began to make a strange noise when I ___ home.
a: The car began to make a strange noise when I was driving home.

q:
watch
 The TV was on but nobody ___ it.
a: The TV was on but nobody was watching it.

q:
work
 When I first met Jessica, she ___ in a clothes shop.
a: She was working in a clothes shop.

q:
see / look
 I ___ Sue in town yesterday, but she ___ the other way.
a: I saw Sue in town yesterday, but she was looking the other way.

q:
cycle / step
 I ___ home yesterday when a man ___ out into the road in front of me.
a: I was cycling home yesterday when a man stepped out into the road in front of me.

q:
go / manage / hit
 She ___ quite fast, but luckily she ___ to stop in time, and she ___ him.
a: She was going quite fast, but luckily she managed to stop in time, and she didn't hit him.

q:
do
 'What ___ at this time yesterday?' 'I was asleep.'
a: What were you doing at this time yesterday?

q:
go
 '___ out last night?' 'No, I was too tired.'
a: Did you go out last night?

q:
drive / happen
 How fast ___ when the accident ___?
a: How fast were you driving when the accident happened?

q:
take / look
 Sam ___ a picture of me while I ___.
a: Sam took a picture of me while I wasn't looking.

q:
know / do
 We ___ what to do, so we ___ nothing.
a: We didn't know what to do, so we did nothing.

q:
see / try
 When I last ___ him, he ___ to find a job.
a: When I last saw him, he was trying to find a job.

q:
walk / hear
 I ___ along the street when suddenly I ___ something behind me.
a: I was walking along the street when suddenly I heard something behind me.

q:
follow / start
 Somebody ___ me. I was scared and I ___ to run.
a: Somebody was following me. I was scared and I started to run.

q:
want / change
 When I was young, I ___ to be a pilot. Later I ___ my mind.
a: When I was young, I wanted to be a pilot. Later I changed my mind.

q:
drop / do / break
 Last night I ___ a plate when I ___ the washing up. Fortunately it ___.
a: Last night I dropped a plate when I was doing the washing up. Fortunately it didn't break.
